testcase.name: test_ask_stories

failure.message: TypeError: hackernews.Item() argument after ** must be a mapping, not int

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_ask_stories.TestAskStories testMethod=test_ask_stories>
def test_ask_stories(self): buffer = BytesIO() c = pycurl.Curl() c.setopt(c.URL, 'https://hacker-news.firebaseio.com/v0/askstories.json?print=pretty') c.setopt(c.WRITEDATA, buffer) c.perform() c.close()
response = json.loads(buffer.getvalue().decode('utf-8')) > ask_stories = [Item(**item) for item in response[:10]] # Assuming Item can be initialized this way ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_ask_stories.py:33: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
.0 = <list_iterator object at 0x105830940>
> ask_stories = [Item(**item) for item in response[:10]] # Assuming Item can be initialized this way ^^^^^^^^^^^^ E TypeError: hackernews.Item() argument after ** must be a mapping, not int
tests/test_ask_stories.py:33: TypeError

testcase.name: test_ask_stories_raw

failure.message: AssertionError: 47282777 is not an instance of <class 'str'>

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_ask_stories.TestAskStories testMethod=test_ask_stories_raw>
def test_ask_stories_raw(self): buffer = BytesIO() c = pycurl.Curl() c.setopt(c.URL, 'https://hacker-news.firebaseio.com/v0/askstories.json?print=pretty') c.setopt(c.WRITEDATA, buffer) c.perform() c.close()
response = buffer.getvalue().decode('utf-8') ask_stories = json.loads(response) self.assertIsInstance(ask_stories, list) > self.assertIsInstance(ask_stories[0], str) E AssertionError: 47282777 is not an instance of <class 'str'>
tests/test_ask_stories.py:49: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_get_item

failure.message: TypeError: Item.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'by'

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_get_item.TestGetItem testMethod=test_get_item>
def test_get_item(self): > item = self.get_item_with_pycurl(8863) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_get_item.py:45: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <tests.test_get_item.TestGetItem testMethod=test_get_item> item_id = 8863, expand = False
def get_item_with_pycurl(self, item_id, expand=False): buffer = BytesIO() c = pycurl.Curl() c.setopt(c.URL, f'https://hacker-news.firebaseio.com/v0/item/{item_id}.json') c.setopt(c.WRITEDATA, buffer) c.perform() c.close() response_data = buffer.getvalue() item_data = json.loads(response_data)
if 'error' in item_data: raise InvalidItemID("Invalid item ID")
> item = Item(**item_data) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E TypeError: Item.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'by'
tests/test_get_item.py:38: TypeError

testcase.name: test_get_max_item_expand

failure.message: AttributeError: type object 'Item' has no attribute 'from_json'

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_get_max_item.TestGetMaxItem testMethod=test_get_max_item_expand>
def test_get_max_item_expand(self): buffer = BytesIO() self.curl.setopt(self.curl.URL, 'https://hacker-news.firebaseio.com/v0/maxitem.json?expand=true') self.curl.setopt(self.curl.WRITEDATA, buffer) self.curl.perform() max_item_data = buffer.getvalue().decode('utf-8') > max_item = Item.from_json(max_item_data) # Assuming Item has a method to create from JSON ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: type object 'Item' has no attribute 'from_json'
tests/test_get_max_item.py:37: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_get_users_by_ids
testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_get_users_by_ids.TestGetUsersByIDs testMethod=test_get_users_by_ids>
def test_get_users_by_ids(self): # Prepare a buffer to capture the response buffer = BytesIO() c = pycurl.Curl() c.setopt(c.URL, 'https://hacker-news.firebaseio.com/v0/user/pg.json') c.setopt(c.WRITEDATA, buffer) c.perform() c.close()
# Decode the response response = buffer.getvalue().decode('utf-8') users = [response] # This is a placeholder; actual implementation will vary
self.assertIsInstance(users, list) > self.assertEqual(len(users), 3) E AssertionError: 1 != 3
tests/test_get_users_by_ids.py:36: AssertionError

self = <tests.test_job_stories.TestJobStories testMethod=test_job_stories>
def test_job_stories(self): buffer = BytesIO() c = pycurl.Curl() c.setopt(c.URL, 'https://hacker-news.firebaseio.com/v0/jobstories.json?limit=10') c.setopt(c.WRITEDATA, buffer) c.perform() c.close()
> job_stories = self.hn.parse_response(buffer.getvalue()) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: 'HackerNews' object has no attribute 'parse_response'

> job_stories = self.hn.parse_response_raw(buffer.getvalue()) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: 'HackerNews' object has no attribute 'parse_response_raw'

testcase.name: test_new_stories_raw

failure.message: AssertionError: 47289976 is not an instance of <class 'str'>

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_new_stories.TestNewStories testMethod=test_new_stories_raw>
def test_new_stories_raw(self): buffer = BytesIO() c = pycurl.Curl() c.setopt(c.URL, 'https://hacker-news.firebaseio.com/v0/newstories.json?print=pretty') c.setopt(c.WRITEDATA, buffer) c.perform() c.close()
body = buffer.getvalue().decode('utf-8') new_stories = eval(body) # Assuming the response is a JSON array of story IDs self.assertIsInstance(new_stories, list) > self.assertIsInstance(new_stories[0], str) E AssertionError: 47289976 is not an instance of <class 'str'>
tests/test_new_stories.py:39: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_top_stories
testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_updates.TestUpdates testMethod=test_top_stories>
def test_top_stories(self): buffer = BytesIO() c = pycurl.Curl() > c.setopt(c.URL, self.hn.api_url + '/updates') # Assuming api_url is defined in HackerNews ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: 'HackerNews' object has no attribute 'api_url'
tests/test_updates.py:24: AttributeError
